title: Lane 2 content richness runway
summary: Run a larger content-richness wave that deepens old-growth, coastal food-web, and high-country science through existing content, atlas, journal, close-look, and ledger seams.

execution_order.goal: Scope an old-growth and under-root content pack that rides existing forest and cave-adjacent surfaces.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 2 can deepen the world without waiting on station or route structure.

execution_order.goal: Implement the old-growth and under-root content pack with science ledger and content tests.
execution_order.why_now: The forest expedition and vertical work need richer educational fuel, not new systems.

execution_order.goal: Review old-growth content for science accuracy, copy budget, and no station or route drift.
execution_order.why_now: Content richness must stay modular and source-safe.

execution_order.goal: Scope a coastal food-web content pack around wrack, dune, scrub, and forest-edge relationships.
execution_order.why_now: The front half should feel as authored and connected as the inland half.

execution_order.goal: Implement the coastal food-web pack using existing notes, prompts, comparison, or close-look seams.
execution_order.why_now: A richer coast helps new players see relationships before later beta chapters.

execution_order.goal: Review coastal content for science safety, readability, and clean front-half balance.
execution_order.why_now: The coastal branch should grow without becoming text-heavy or system-heavy.

execution_order.goal: Scope a high-country season and shelter content pack for Treeline/Tundra.
execution_order.why_now: High-country content can deepen the beta's source-side identity independently from route work.

execution_order.goal: Implement the high-country content pack with focused content-quality and ledger checks.
execution_order.why_now: Treeline and tundra should carry stronger science density before more chapter breadth opens.

execution_order.goal: Review high-country content for source safety, copy fit, and no route/station dependency.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 2 should finish with richer content that future lanes can use without waiting on it.

completion_gate: Old-growth, coastal, and high-country content packs each land through existing content-facing systems.
Science-source ledger and content-quality checks stay current.
No station shell, route-board layout, route catalog semantics, world-map orchestration, or broad `game.ts` work lands in lane 2.
Focused content/journal/close-look tests and build pass when runtime-visible content changes land.
guardrails: Lane 2 owns content richness, not progression structure.
Use existing journal, atlas, close-look, sketchbook, prompt, and ecosystem-note carriers.
Keep facts short, source-safe, and kid-readable.
Leave station, route, travel, and traversal implementation requests as handoffs for other lanes.
Do not add a fourth Source to Shore beat or a new content shell.
post_packet_path: If clean, lane 2 can propose a later atlas/sketchbook synthesis packet.
Any system need discovered here should become a handoff, not an absorbed dependency.

scout_466_handoff.selected_scope: A compact Forest Trail decomposition pack with exactly two new landmark inspectables, one old-growth close-look seed, two ecosystem notes, and science-ledger coverage.
implementation_contract: Add `leaf-litter-pocket` and `shelf-fungus` as forest landmark entries in `src/content/biomes/forest.ts`.
Add small `leaf-litter-pocket` and `shelf-fungus` sprites to `src/assets/forest-ambient.ts`; register no new asset module.
Use authored placements only: `leaf-litter-pocket` in the existing under-root/stone-basin cluster, and `shelf-fungus` on an existing old-growth log/trunk surface.
Add a `shelf-fungus` close-look seed in `src/engine/close-look.ts`; keep `leaf-litter-pocket` out of close-look unless readability needs it.
Add ecosystem notes `old-wood-recyclers` and `leaf-litter-shelter` using existing note resolver behavior.
Update `docs/science-source-ledger.md` with broad `Watch` rows for both landmarks using official NPS fungi/decomposition sources.
out_of_scope: No station shell, route board, route catalog, field request, support behavior, world-map, corridor, save-schema, or broad `game.ts` edits.
No new cave route, old-growth route, comparison shell, atlas page, or fourth Source to Shore beat.

implemented_scope: Added `leaf-litter-pocket` and `shelf-fungus` as Forest Trail landmark inspectables.
Added compact forest ambient sprites for both entries without a new asset module.
Placed `leaf-litter-pocket` in the under-root stone-basin cluster and `shelf-fungus` on an existing old-growth trunk/log surface.
Updated science-ledger coverage and focused tests.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/ecosystem-notes.test.ts src/test/close-look.test.ts src/test/content-quality.test.ts

critic_466_review.review_summary: The old-growth and under-root content pack is source-safe, compact, and lane-local. It adds two broad landmark inspectables, authored placements, one close-look seed, two ecosystem notes, ledger rows, and focused tests without station, route, save, geometry, or new-shell drift.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/ecosystem-notes.test.ts src/test/close-look.test.ts src/test/content-quality.test.ts

scout_467_handoff.selected_scope: A notes-only coastal food-web pack that adds exactly three ecosystem notes across Beach, Coastal Scrub, and Forest Trail using existing entries and authored placements.
implementation_contract: Add Beach ecosystem note `wrack-bird-line` for `bull-kelp-wrack`, `beach-hopper`, and `western-snowy-plover` in zone `tide-line`.
Add Coastal Scrub ecosystem note `berry-cover-chain` for `beach-strawberry`, `nootka-rose`, `salmonberry`, and `deer-mouse` with no zone id because it spans swale, thicket, and forest-edge pockets.
Add Forest Trail ecosystem note `berry-seed-shuttle` for `steller-jay`, `red-huckleberry`, and `salmonberry` in zone `trailhead`.
Keep all note copy compact, broad, and kid-readable; avoid new specialist diet, nesting, migration, or guaranteed seed-dispersal claims.
Do not add inspectables, sprites, close-look seeds, comparison shell work, route beats, station UI, world-map behavior, save state, geometry, or broad runtime changes.

note_contract.title: Wrack Bird Line
note_contract.summary: Kelp wrack feeds hoppers and other tiny animals that small shorebirds watch for.
note_contract.observation_prompt: Where does wrack become bird food?

note_contract.title: Berry Cover Chain
note_contract.summary: Low fruit, rose hips, and edge berries give small mammals food beside cover.
note_contract.observation_prompt: Where do berries sit close to cover?

note_contract.title: Berry Seed Shuttle
note_contract.summary: Berries feed forest birds, and some seeds travel away from the edge.
note_contract.observation_prompt: Which berries might a bird carry next?

proof_expectations: npm test -- --run src/test/ecosystem-notes.test.ts src/test/beach-biome.test.ts src/test/coastal-scrub-biome.test.ts src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/content-quality.test.ts
out_of_scope: No new inspectables, sprites, asset modules, close-look seeds, comparison allowlists, or comparison shells.
No station shell, route board, route catalog, field requests, support behavior, world-map, save-schema, geometry, traversal, or broad `game.ts` edits.
No new route beats, fourth Source to Shore beat, new page, badge, or reward economy.

implemented_scope: Added Beach ecosystem note `wrack-bird-line` for `bull-kelp-wrack`, `beach-hopper`, and `western-snowy-plover`.
Added Coastal Scrub ecosystem note `berry-cover-chain` for `beach-strawberry`, `nootka-rose`, `salmonberry`, and `deer-mouse`.
Added Forest Trail ecosystem note `berry-seed-shuttle` for `steller-jay`, `red-huckleberry`, and `salmonberry`.
Added focused ecosystem-note and biome assertions while relying on existing content-quality ledger coverage.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/ecosystem-notes.test.ts src/test/beach-biome.test.ts src/test/coastal-scrub-biome.test.ts src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/content-quality.test.ts
implementation_467_result.ledger_note: No ledger edit was needed because the notes use existing ledger-backed entries and avoid new specialist diet, nesting, migration, or guaranteed seed-dispersal claims.

critic_467_review.review_summary: The coastal food-web pack is source-safe, compact, and lane-local. It adds exactly three ecosystem notes through existing biome content and focused tests, with no station, route, world-map, save, geometry, close-look, comparison-shell, reward, page, or fourth Source to Shore drift.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/ecosystem-notes.test.ts src/test/beach-biome.test.ts src/test/coastal-scrub-biome.test.ts src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/content-quality.test.ts

scout_468_handoff.selected_scope: A one-seed Tundra close-look pass for `woolly-lousewort`, scoped down from more ecosystem-note copy because Treeline and Tundra already have dense note coverage.
implementation_contract: Add exactly one close-look seed for `woolly-lousewort` in `src/engine/close-look.ts`.
Use sentence `Woolly fuzz helps hold warm air around the stem in cold tundra wind.`
Update `src/test/close-look.test.ts` allowlist and payload assertions for `woolly-lousewort`.
Run `src/test/content-quality.test.ts`; do not edit the science ledger unless that guard shows the existing verified row is missing or stale.
out_of_scope: No new inspectables, sprites, asset modules, ecosystem notes, comparison allowlists, sketchbook shell, atlas page, station UI, route catalog semantics, route beats, support behavior, world-map behavior, save-schema changes, geometry, or broad `game.ts` edits.
No fourth Source to Shore beat, badge, reward economy, or new page.
No browser proof is required unless review finds close-look card cropping or readability risk.

implemented_scope: Added exactly one close-look seed for `woolly-lousewort` in `src/engine/close-look.ts`.
Used sentence `Woolly fuzz helps hold warm air around the stem in cold tundra wind.`
implementation_468_result.ledger_note: No science-ledger edit was needed because `woolly-lousewort` already has a verified ledger row and the new copy stays inside that source-backed woolly-stem insulation claim.

critic_468_review.review_summary: The high-country close-look pass is source-safe, compact, and lane-local. It adds exactly one `woolly-lousewort` close-look seed backed by the existing verified ledger row, with no inspectable, sprite, note, comparison, station, route, support, world-map, save, geometry, or broad runtime drift.
